title: Altering & Dropping
description: Making structural changes later, or destroying tables.

slides.title: I forgot a column! 🤦‍♂️
slides.content:
# The ALTER TABLE command

You built the `users` table, people are using the app... and suddenly your boss says "We need to track their phone numbers!"

You can't recreate the table (you'd lose data). Instead, you **alter** it structurally.

`ALTER TABLE users ADD phone_number VARCHAR(20);`

quizData.question: What does ALTER TABLE inherently achieve?

options.text: It modifies the physical foundational blueprint (the schema) of an already existing table, like adding brand new columns

quizData.explanation: ALTER changes the building footprint. UPDATE changes the furniture inside.

slides.title: Burn it down 🔥
slides.content:
## The DROP TABLE command

If a table is completely obsolete or you made a critical mistake in development, you can completely physically delete the entire table structure (and natively, all its internal data simultaneously) using one terrifying command.

`DROP TABLE old_analytics;`

quizData.question: What is the extreme philosophical difference between DELETE FROM and DROP TABLE?

options.text: DELETE erases the internal rows but leaves the empty box intact. DROP incinerates the entire box (the schema itself) permanently.
DROP deletes a single column strictly

quizData.explanation: DROP is the ultimate structural eraser. The entire table vanishes from the schema.
